title: Prune Fine Sky-Bin Feature Family
summary: Remove the high-cardinality fine sky-bin categorical family and its frequency features from the current feature set.
feature_strategy: Keep the core photometric, redshift, coarse sky-bin, galactic, rank, and auxiliary features. Drop sky_alpha_bin_48, sky_delta_bin_36, sky_cell_bin_48, their spectral/galaxy interaction categories, and their covariate-only frequency columns before encoding/model fitting.
baseline_model_panel: Use the current CatBoost, XGBoost, and LightGBM ensemble, fold-wise blend, and fold-wise calibration with the pruned feature matrix. No model hyperparameters are changed.
model_panel_rationale: The feature stack is broad and some fine sky-bin crosses may be noisy or overly transductive. Pruning this high-cardinality block tests whether the ensemble generalizes better with less spatial memorization.
validation_strategy: Use the same 5-fold competition-row OOF protocol and balanced accuracy. Train+test covariate-only features remain label-free; the only change is dropping a named fine-bin block before categorical handling and numeric median filling.
materialization_hint: Start from response.py. Define PRUNED_FINE_SKY_FEATURES, remove these names from CATEGORICAL_COLS, and after add_all_features drop any matching columns from train_full and test before categorical normalization. Preserve all models, sample weights, blend search, calibration, and artifact writing.
expected_signal: If fine sky bins are mostly noise or overfit, OOF balanced accuracy should hold or increase despite fewer features. If they encode real synthetic-generation structure, the score should fall, confirming their importance.
risk: The parent feature recipe may rely on fine sky localization, so this could reduce accuracy. The result is still useful as an ablation for feature-stack pruning.
rationale:
Hypothesis 000034: Remove the high-cardinality fine sky-bin categorical family and its frequency features from the current feature set.
Parent pipeline score reference: 0.968002 OOF balanced accuracy.
This experiment is intentionally atomic so the score movement can be attributed to the named change.
implementation_hint: Start from response.py. Define PRUNED_FINE_SKY_FEATURES, remove these names from CATEGORICAL_COLS, and after add_all_features drop any matching columns from train_full and test before categorical normalization. Preserve all models, sample weights, blend search, calibration, and artifact writing.
expected_effect: If fine sky bins are mostly noise or overfit, OOF balanced accuracy should hold or increase despite fewer features. If they encode real synthetic-generation structure, the score should fall, confirming their importance.

parent_cv_reference.note: Use this hypothesis run score, not the parent score, as the empirical result.
